title: SPARQL + Cypher — query & create graphs
description: Query RDF with SPARQL; create/read Neo4j with parameterized Cypher; shortestPath for retrieval.
story: SPARQL is SQL-for-triples on RDF. Cypher is pattern-matching on property graphs. Same diagnosis idea: find Symptom → FailureMode → Part. In production we parameterize every id ($product_id) so plans cache and injection fails closed.
one_liner: SPARQL for RDF; Cypher for Neo4j; always parameterize; prefer typed short paths over full scans.

beats.title: SPARQL — symptoms indicating failure modes
beats.narrative: Graph pattern in WHERE mirrors the triples you asserted.

annotations.note: Triple patterns share variables to join.

beats.title: Cypher CREATE/MERGE path
beats.narrative: MERGE is upsert. Properties on INDICATES store likelihood.
beats.code:
// Create path (parameterize ids in real apps)
MERGE (p:Product {product_id: $product_id})
  ON CREATE SET p.name = $name
MERGE (s:Symptom {symptom_id: $symptom_id})
  ON CREATE SET s.description = $description
MERGE (fm:FailureMode {failure_mode_id: $fm_id})
  ON CREATE SET fm.name = $fm_name
MERGE (p)-[:HAS_SYMPTOM]->(s)
MERGE (s)-[:INDICATES {confidence: $confidence}]->(fm)
RETURN p, s, fm

annotations.note: MERGE Product by business key product_id.
$params — never f-string user text into Cypher.

beats.title: Cypher retrieval for ranking (GraphRAG style)
beats.narrative: Product-scoped candidates only — never global symptom scan first.
beats.code:
MATCH (p:Product {product_id: $product_id})-[:CAN_HAVE]->(fm:FailureMode)
OPTIONAL MATCH (s:Symptom)-[ind:INDICATES]->(fm)
WHERE s.symptom_id IN $symptom_ids
WITH fm, collect({symptom_id: s.symptom_id, confidence: ind.confidence}) AS indications,
     sum(coalesce(ind.confidence, 0)) AS total_confidence
RETURN fm.failure_mode_id AS failure_mode_id,
       fm.name AS name,
       indications,
       total_confidence
ORDER BY total_confidence DESC

annotations.note: Start at Product — partitions the search space.
ORDER BY total_confidence — pre-rank before Bayes in Python.

beats.title: shortestPath for compact diagnostic trails
beats.narrative: Bounded variable-length paths keep retrieval fast and explainable.
beats.code:
// Shortest diagnostic path: symptom → FM → step → part
MATCH (s:Symptom {symptom_id: $sid})-[:INDICATES]->(fm:FailureMode)
MATCH path = shortestPath(
  (fm)-[:CONFIRMS|REQUIRES_PART*1..4]-(part:Part)
)
RETURN path
LIMIT 5

annotations.note: Relationship type union + max depth 4 bounds cost.

concepts.definition: Minimal hop path in property graph

self_quiz.a: SELECT variables; WHERE holds graph patterns.
Product scope → typed edges → rank → optional shortestPath explain.

common_mistakes: Global MATCH (s:Symptom) without product filter on large graphs.
Putting ranking math in Cypher when Python Bayes is clearer/testable.
final_boss: Write SPARQL that returns symptom labels for a failure mode.
Explain shortestPath bounds for production safety.
